transcript.clean_text: sunny skies greeted the Peloton of parinees as they headed from north to south the longest day and a transitional stage 212.4 kilometers heading towards sample Dua Chateau three early King of the mountains climbs one late one and an intermediate Sprint as well with bonus seconds of six four and two available inside the last 50 kilometers it will be a headwind for most of the day putting off attacks two Riders would go up the road but they wouldn't last long at all 80 kilometers from the finish both Jonas craigo had taken maximum points in the king of the mountains classification and Sunday du jardan were pulled back when we got to the intermediate Sprint there was plenty of action though it was short and very intense David godw picking it up album Demar in second and for the first time this week unable to do the business when he'd gone for it that meant that there would be a four second swing in favor of David gordu he now trailed by six seconds in the general classification however tarepogancha taking two seconds on the rest of his Rivals as Jonas cregor made it 16 points out of a possible 16 and extended his own lead in the king of the mountains classification on pogacha to 18 with three races to go into the final kilometers of this race which doesn't have a long highlight Trill as you can see quieter stay for quite a few years on parkinis by the time they were in the final three kilometers however it was a real fight nervous one for positioning there were roundabouts and traffic islands all over the place another very very unnecessarily dangerous finish for the Riders perhaps dodging left and right missing Wheels here and there it'll be an attack from Ben Swift in the final 700 meters that would really set things going try to pull off an unlikely attacking win but Eduardo afini pulled him back inside the final of the roundabouts just 300 meters to go Mass Peterson then launched the Sprint but it would be Eduardo afini's teammate Olaf koi who at 21 years of age would hit the front stay at the front and take the biggest victory of just the second year of his young career Sprint beating some of the biggest sprinters in the world and announcing his arrival out of the chaos as the next big yon Sprint Sensation from the Netherlands second place would go to Pearson a third on the day to Med lead with trentine up there Cantor in the top five as well and ball made up the top ten all the headlines though to Yambo visman olafcoy who rebounded after a disappointment the day before that soughtare pogacha take yellow he'll keep yellow and have a lead of six seconds going into the final three stages Wenger is now at 46 Yates at 58 with murder at one minute and 21. so to Stage six to off to La col solu almost 200 kilometers a long difficult day up down left and right one perhaps for a breakaway maybe even the general classification Riders could get interested as well it's an unpredictable day into the hills but very much firmly in the south of France now and a final loop around La culciolu a finish after 197.4 kilometers and one that you'll be able to see on GCM plus live or on demand remember running concurrently this week on the home of cycling foreign
